meta.description: Agent loading guide for the MSM Portal Design System. Tells AI agents which files and sections to read for each task type.

tokens.json.description: Design tokens: colors, spacing, typography, elevation, border radius, layout constants. All accessed via theme.mcui.* in styled-components.
tokens.json.agent_value: Prevents hardcoded values. Every visual property (color, spacing, font size) must reference a token via props.theme.mcui.*.

components.json.description: Component catalog across 8 categories: Form Inputs (v1), Form Shared, Form Layout, Buttons, Navigation, Feedback & Overlay, Table, Layout — with props, when_to_use, do_not_use, states, and compositions.
components.json.agent_value: Prevents invented props and wrong component choices. Always check when_to_use and do_not_use before selecting a component.

patterns.json.description: 20 composition patterns with code examples. Key patterns include: form-basic, form-full-page, list-page, detail-page, create-page, edit-page, delete-confirm-dialog, styled-component, action-button, tab-navigation, trpc-data-fetching, provider-stack, field-layout, page-container-component, error-handling, loading-state, route-registration, i18n-usage, accessibility, navigation.
patterns.json.agent_value: Enables scaffolding complete features. Patterns with layer_structure and file_checklist define every file to create and each layer's responsibility.

conventions.json.description: Naming rules (MC/MT/SC/ME/use prefixes), file structure, import aliases, architecture, import order, and machine-checkable constraints.
conventions.json.agent_value: Prevents convention violations. The constraints array enables self-validation of generated code before delivery.

api-ui-contracts.json.description: Entity-level API contracts mapping Proto types to converter files to Model fields to UI components and table columns.
api-ui-contracts.json.agent_value: Grounds data fetching code in reality. Shows exact converter files, field mappings, API endpoints, and which table columns come from which API fields.

component-behaviors.json.description: Semantic actions and data flow for each component — what users DO and how that maps to Formik/React events. Organized by formInputs, buttons, table, navigation, dialog.
component-behaviors.json.agent_value: Encodes user intent (not just technical events). Helps agents generate correct interaction handling code for each component type.

state-machines.json.description: State transition definitions for complex interactive components, starting with the shared MCForm* base machine and individual component extensions.
state-machines.json.agent_value: Defines valid state transitions and their triggers. Prevents impossible state combinations (e.g., error shown before touch).

pm-sa-request-schema.json.description: Structured request schema and form specification for PM/SA driven UI changes. Defines the minimum fields needed to safely turn product requests into agent work items.
pm-sa-request-schema.json.agent_value: Reduces ambiguity in free-form requests. Helps agents identify goal, target page, intent type, success criteria, and validation expectations before editing code.

preview-verification.json.description: Preview verification contract for runtime review. Defines what must match before a preview is safe to show: client, route, language, screenshot readiness, and intent-specific checks.
preview-verification.json.agent_value: Turns preview correctness into machine-readable rules. Prevents wrong-client, wrong-language, and invisible-copy previews from reaching PM/SA review.

golden-example-states.json.description: Golden example states for key components. Documents the canonical states that should be visible in docs, previews, and review checks.
golden-example-states.json.agent_value: Defines what good looks like for major components. Helps both humans and agents review default, error, disabled, loading, and active states consistently.

architecture.md.description: Component architecture: 3-layer structure (Library → Wrapper → App), Formik wrapping pattern, Provider stack, getTheme() utility, MEFormFieldWidth enum, MCFormLayout anatomy.
architecture.md.agent_value: Essential context for understanding where components live. Prevents using Layer 1 primitives directly in forms (must use Layer 2 wrappers). Documents the onChange signature difference (event vs value) that causes runtime errors.

migration-status.md.description: Component migration status: MCButton→MCButton2 transition (88% done, 15 files remaining), deprecated component conversion (MCModal 18 files remaining), brand color correction (#346bea not #6360DC).
migration-status.md.agent_value: Prevents using deprecated components in new code. When generating button code, always use MCButton2 (variant=basic, color=error) not MCButton (variant=contained, color=danger).

ux-writing.json.description: UX writing guidance for labels, placeholders, dialogs, errors, empty states, and toasts. Includes tone rules, examples, and machine-readable checks for wording consistency.
ux-writing.json.agent_value: Keeps product writing clear, consistent, and professional. Helps agents choose action-specific labels, recovery-oriented errors, and consistent terminology.

building_a_new_page.description: Scaffolding a completely new feature page (list, detail, create, or edit)
load_order: conventions.json → architecture, namingPrefixes, fileNaming, importAliases
patterns.json → find matching pattern (list-page, detail-page, create-page, edit-page, page-container-component)
api-ui-contracts.json → find entity field mappings and endpoints
components.json → select components based on when_to_use
tokens.json → spacing and typography for styled-components

interpreting_pm_sa_request.description: Turning a PM/SA improvement request into a safe engineering task
load_order: pm-sa-request-schema.json → confirm required fields and intent type
preview-verification.json → understand what must be true before preview is reviewable
index.json → choose the matching task type
components.json → find likely target component
patterns.json → confirm composition pattern
interpreting_pm_sa_request.critical_check: The request must include client, route, intent type, and success criteria before editing code

implementing_a_form.description: Building a form with validation, field groups, panels, and submission
load_order: patterns.json → form-basic or form-full-page or create-page or edit-page
components.json → Form Inputs (v1) and Form Shared categories
state-machines.json → formInputs shared base machine
component-behaviors.json → formInputs semantic actions and data flow
conventions.json → constraints (Formik context rule)
tokens.json → spacing for form layout
implementing_a_form.critical_check: All MCForm* components must be inside a <Formik> context

styling_a_component.description: Applying visual styles using design tokens and styled-components
load_order: tokens.json → color, spacing, typography, borderRadius, elevation
conventions.json → constraints (no hardcoded hex/px values), styledComponentRules
patterns.json → styled-component pattern
components.json → check component's structure field for expected dimensions/padding
styling_a_component.critical_check: No hardcoded hex or px values — see conventions.json#constraints

adding_a_table_view.description: Implementing a data table with columns, rows, and row-level actions
load_order: api-ui-contracts.json → entity tableColumns and field mappings
components.json → Table category (MCTable, MCDataTable)
patterns.json → list-page
component-behaviors.json → table interactions
patterns.json → action-button (for row-level actions)
adding_a_table_view.critical_check: Column definitions must match API contract field names

handling_user_interactions.description: Implementing buttons, dialogs, navigation, and user action flows
load_order: component-behaviors.json → find component's semantic_actions
state-machines.json → valid state transitions
components.json → component props and available compositions
patterns.json → delete-confirm-dialog, action-button, tab-navigation, navigation
handling_user_interactions.critical_check: Match semantic action to correct component — check do_not_use in components.json

connecting_api_to_ui.description: Wiring tRPC/backend data to frontend Container and Component layers
load_order: api-ui-contracts.json → entity definition, converter files, field mappings, endpoints
patterns.json → trpc-data-fetching, provider-stack
components.json → data display components (Table, Layout categories)
conventions.json → architecture section (Container handles data fetching, not Component)
connecting_api_to_ui.critical_check: Data fetching must happen in Container layer only — never in Component

adding_i18n_strings.description: Adding user-facing strings with translation support
load_order: patterns.json → i18n-usage
ux-writing.json → service_voice, surface_rules, examples
conventions.json → constraints (no raw strings rule)
adding_i18n_strings.critical_check: All user-facing strings must use useTranslation — and the resulting copy should follow ux-writing.json rules for tone, specificity, and recovery guidance

registering_a_route.description: Adding a new page to the app's routing configuration
load_order: patterns.json → route-registration
conventions.json → importAliases, architecture
registering_a_route.critical_check: Use MERouteKey enum for route identifiers

handling_errors_and_loading.description: Implementing loading skeletons and error boundary states
load_order: patterns.json → error-handling, loading-state
components.json → Feedback & Overlay category
component-behaviors.json → relevant component error states
handling_errors_and_loading.critical_check: Loading and error states must be handled in Container, not Component

code_review.description: Reviewing generated or written UI code for correctness and convention compliance
load_order: conventions.json → constraints (all rules)
components.json → verify correct component usage, props, and when_to_use
tokens.json → verify no hardcoded color/spacing/font values
ux-writing.json → tone, CTA, error, and empty-state guidance
patterns.json → verify correct architecture pattern and layer boundaries
code_review.critical_check: All constraints in conventions.json must pass

reviewing_a_preview.description: Verifying that a generated preview is safe to show to PM/SA reviewers
load_order: preview-verification.json → global_rules and intent_rules
ux-writing.json → surface_rules and examples for visible copy
golden-example-states.json → understand the expected visible state
components.json → check component-specific states and examples
../workflows/visual-inspection.json → compare screenshot structure and hierarchy
reviewing_a_preview.critical_check: Preview client, route, language, and change visibility must match the request before review

modifying_existing_page.description: Changing behavior, layout, or data display on an existing page. Not building from scratch.
load_order: api-ui-contracts.json → find the entity contract to understand existing field mappings, converters, and container paths
conventions.json → architecture rules and constraints — verify the current code follows them before changing
patterns.json → identify which pattern the page currently uses (list-page, detail-page, create-page, edit-page)
components.json → verify the target component's props, when_to_use, do_not_use before swapping or adding components
tokens.json → if the change involves visual properties, verify correct token usage
../workflows/validation-runner.json → run checks after modification to catch regressions
modifying_existing_page.critical_check: Read the existing Container and Component files BEFORE making changes. Identify which layer (Page/Container/Component) the change belongs to. Never add data fetching to a Component or UI logic to a Container.

decision_tree.change_is_data_or_api: Start with api-ui-contracts.json → find converter file → trace field from proto to model to UI component
decision_tree.change_is_layout_or_styling: Start with tokens.json → verify token names → check component props for layout variants
decision_tree.change_is_adding_component: Start with components.json → check when_to_use and do_not_use → check component-dependencies.json for required providers
decision_tree.change_is_form_field: Start with components.json → find the input component → check formikRequired flag → read the Container to find the Yup schema

debugging_existing_page.description: Diagnosing and fixing a bug in an existing page. The page renders incorrectly, throws errors, or behaves unexpectedly.
load_order: ../workflows/error-patterns.json → check if this is a known error pattern with a documented fix
conventions.json → verify the code follows architecture constraints (most bugs come from constraint violations)
component-dependencies.json → check if all required providers are present
state-machines.json → verify the component is in a valid state and transitions are correct
api-ui-contracts.json → if data-related, trace the field from proto → converter → model → UI
debugging_existing_page.critical_check: Before fixing: identify the 3-layer file set (Page/Container/Component). The bug might be in a different layer than where the symptom appears. A missing field in UI usually means the Converter is not mapping it, not that the Component is broken.

decision_tree.runtime_error: Check ../workflows/error-patterns.json by error message → apply documented fix
decision_tree.build_error: Check ../workflows/error-patterns.json build section → verify imports and type compatibility
decision_tree.wrong_data_displayed: Trace through api-ui-contracts.json: proto field → converter → model field → component prop
decision_tree.styling_issue: Check tokens.json for correct token → verify no hardcoded colors (conventions.json constraint)
decision_tree.state_issue: Check state-machines.json for the component's valid states and transitions

understanding_existing_code.description: Given a file path or feature name, understand how the code is structured, what data it uses, and how it relates to the design system.
load_order: conventions.json → understand the Page/Container/Component architecture and naming conventions
api-ui-contracts.json → find which entity this code belongs to and trace the data pipeline
patterns.json → identify which pattern this code implements
component-dependencies.json → understand what providers and contexts are needed
components.json → look up any MC-prefixed components used in the code
understanding_existing_code.critical_check: MSM Portal follows a strict 3-layer architecture: Page (route + layout), Container (data fetching + business logic), Component (pure UI). Given a file path, first determine which layer it belongs to by checking the path pattern: */container/* = Container layer, */component/* = Component layer, pages/routes = Page layer.

decision_tree.given_file_path: Determine layer from path → read the file → identify imports to find related Container/Component files → check api-ui-contracts.json for entity context
decision_tree.given_feature_name: Search api-ui-contracts.json for the entity → find container paths → read Container to find Component imports → read Component for UI structure
decision_tree.given_component_name: Look up in components.json → check component-dependencies.json → find which Containers import this component

page_level_alert.component: MCAlert or MCBanner — check components.json#Feedback & Overlay
